title: Node Process, Event Loop, Modules, Configuration, and Lifecycle · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A backend pairing room predicts runtime, request, middleware, dependency, response, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first process, event-loop, configuration, and lifecycle state model for node process, event loop, modules, configuration, and lifecycle. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of process, event-loop, configuration, and lifecycle state model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pra…
objectives: Trace call stack, timers, pending callbacks, poll, check, close callbacks, microtasks, and next-tick work without assuming one request owns one event-loop turn.
Configure ESM, CommonJS interop, package exports, file extensions, and NodeNext resolution without relying on accidental development-loader behavior.
Parse, validate, normalize, redact, and freeze configuration once before acquiring resources or accepting traffic.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Event Loop Work Queues
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop team handles case 2512 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate configuration, readiness, admission, and signal-driven draining; retain compatibility with one older generated client; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Event Loop Work Queues.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace call stack, timers, pending callbacks, poll, check, close callbacks, microtasks, and next-tick work without assuming one request owns one event-loop turn. For Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Event Loop Work Queues, keep Event Loop Work Queues connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
